MERIDIAN

◈ Deal Intelligence ◈

Institutional-grade merger arbitrage intelligence, sourced directly from SEC EDGAR. Every active deal tracked, scored, and updated hourly so you can act with conviction.

Data Refresh Cycle
SEC
Primary Data Source
◈ Proprietary Scoring Model · V2
93.1% accuracy at predicting whether a deal closes or breaks — backtested on 159 historical deals from 2020–2025. Read the full methodology.
→
The Scoring System
◈ How Meridian evaluates every deal
Very Low
0–8% Spread · Score 80+
0% break rate across 116 historical deals. Market has high conviction. These are the positions institutional arb funds hold with maximum confidence.
Low
0–8% Spread · Score 65–79
Tight spread with minor score uncertainty. Still strong risk-adjusted returns. Monitor for any change in deal terms or regulatory news.
Medium
8–12% Spread · Score 75+
36% historical break rate in this spread range. Meaningful market skepticism priced in. Proceed with careful position sizing.
High
12%+ Spread · or Score Below 75
87% break rate above 12% spread. Market is pricing in near-certain deal failure. Approach only with full understanding of downside risk.
01
SEC EDGAR Monitoring
Every hour, Meridian scans 8-K filings for definitive merger agreements, automatically extracting deal price, acquirer, and terms directly from SEC filings.
02
Near Real-Time Spread Calculation
Market prices updated hourly via Yahoo Finance. The spread is the gap between market price and deal price — your potential return if the deal closes.
03
V2 Scoring Model
Our model uses empirical spread-based break rates from 159 historical deals. Deals under 8% spread have shown a 0% break rate. Above 12%, break rate exceeds 87%.
Meridian · Built by Kaushal Koduru
Data · SEC EDGAR / Yahoo Finance
Backtested · 159 historical deals · 2020–2025
For informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Spreads reflect Yahoo Finance data which may be delayed up to 15 minutes. Past model performance does not guarantee future results. Always conduct your own due diligence before making investment decisions.

For informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Spreads reflect Yahoo Finance data which may be delayed up to 15 minutes. Past model performance does not guarantee future results. Always conduct your own due diligence before making investment decisions.
The Scoring Model
◈ V2 Methodology & Backtest Results

Meridian's V2 scoring model combines a base score with empirical spread-based break rates derived from 159 historical deals. The key insight: spread range alone predicts deal outcomes with remarkable accuracy.

93.1%
Overall Accuracy
Predicting deal close or break · 159 deals
159
Deals Backtested
Historical deals from 2020 through 2025
94.5%
Closed Deal Accuracy
120 of 127 closed deals correctly predicted
87.5%
Broken Deal Accuracy
28 of 32 broken deals correctly predicted
◈ Model Lift Over Base Rate · Average Annualized Return
~73%
Historical M&A Close Rate
100%
Very Low Risk Close Rate · 0–8% Spread
+8.2%
Avg Annualized Return on Closed Deals
Historically approximately 73% of announced M&A deals close. Meridian's Very Low Risk bucket — deals with spreads under 8% — showed a 0% break rate across 116 historical deals. The average annualized return on correctly predicted closed deals was 8.2%. These numbers reflect historical backtesting and do not guarantee future performance.
01
The Key Insight — Spread Predicts Outcome
Empirical Break Rates from 159 Historical Deals

The V2 model is built on a simple but powerful empirical finding: the spread range at deal announcement is the strongest predictor of whether a deal closes or breaks. Below 8%, no deals in our dataset broke. Above 12%, 87% broke.

Spread RangeDeals in DatasetBrokenBreak RateV2 Classification
0% to 3%33 deals0 broken0.0%Very Low Risk
3% to 5%62 deals0 broken0.0%Very Low / Low Risk
5% to 8%21 deals0 broken0.0%Very Low / Low Risk
8% to 12%11 deals4 broken36.4%Medium / High Risk
12% to 18%15 deals13 broken86.7%High Risk
18% to 25%12 deals11 broken91.7%High Risk
25%+5 deals4 broken80.0%High Risk
02
Base Score Calculation
Three Factors · Base Score 70 · Used as Tiebreaker in Gray Zone

The base score starts at 70 and is adjusted by three factors. For deals with spreads under 8%, the score determines whether risk is Very Low or Low. For deals in the 8-12% gray zone, the score determines whether risk is Medium or High.

FactorConditionAdjustment
Deal TypeAll Cash+10 points
Deal TypeTender Offer+8 points
Deal TypePrivate Equity+5 points
Deal TypeCash + Stock+0 points
Spread Quality0% to 3%+25 points
Spread Quality3% to 5%+18 points
Spread Quality5% to 8%+10 points
Spread Quality8% to 12%+0 points
Spread Quality12% to 18%-15 points
Spread Quality18% to 25%-25 points
Spread Quality25%+-35 points
Time PendingUnder 90 days+10 points
Time Pending90 to 270 days+0 points
Time Pending270 to 500 days-5 points
Time Pending500+ days-15 points
03
Risk Classification Logic
V2 Model · Spread Rules Override Score

Spread range determines the risk bucket first. The base score is used as a secondary tiebreaker within the 8-12% gray zone. This approach produced 93.1% accuracy versus 88.7% with score-only classification.

Very Low
Spread under 8% · Score 80+
0% break rate across 116 historical deals. Market has maximum conviction this deal closes.
Low
Spread under 8% · Score 65–79
0% break rate historically. Minor score uncertainty. Still strong risk-adjusted returns.
Medium
Spread 8–12% · Score 75+
36% break rate in this range. Some confidence from score but meaningful market skepticism.
High
Spread 12%+ · or Score below 75 with 8–12% spread
87%+ break rate above 12%. Market pricing in near-certain deal failure.
04
Sample Backtest Results
Selected deals · 2020–2025 · V2 Model
TickerSpreadScoreV2 RiskOutcomeAnn. ReturnCorrect
CACC4.0%98Very LowClosed+8.7%✓
GTES3.0%98Very LowClosed+9.4%✓
NUAN2.0%95Very LowClosed+2.0%✓
SGEN5.0%85Very LowClosed+6.6%✓
PING3.0%93Very LowClosed+5.5%✓
SAVE35.0%30HighBrokenN/A✓
SGFY12.0%60HighBrokenN/A✓
CHNG22.0%40HighBrokenN/A✓
SGMO19.0%40HighBrokenN/A✓
ATVI25.0%30HighClosed+3.8%✗
ACI15.0%50HighClosed+6.2%✗

The V2 model's primary limitation is the same as V1 — it cannot distinguish between deals that are genuinely breaking and deals facing regulatory headwinds that eventually close. ATVI and ACI both had wide spreads driven by antitrust risk, were correctly flagged as High Risk, but ultimately closed after prolonged battles. Notably, both still returned positive annualized returns to investors who held through the uncertainty. The model's purpose is risk identification, not absolute prediction.

Meridian · Built by Kaushal Koduru
Backtested · 159 historical deals · 2020–2025
Model V2 · May 2026
For informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past model performance does not guarantee future results. Always conduct your own due diligence before making investment decisions.
